pi_items.source: File analysis: setup_task.sh generates this name in student_iep_data.json

pi_items.verified_value: DeKalb County School District is a real, existing school district in Georgia serving 92,000+ students
pi_items.source: Web search: https://www.dekalbschoolsga.org/ confirms it exists as of 2026

pi_items.verified_value: Lakewood Heights Elementary was a real Atlanta Public Schools building (not DeKalb County), designed in 1932, but was shuttered in 2004. The task incorrectly places it in DeKalb County and treats it as a current operating school.
pi_items.source: Web search results show Lakewood Heights is an Atlanta Public Schools facility being redeveloped, not currently operating in DeKalb County

pi_items.verified_value: Georgia (accurate for DeKalb County School District location)
pi_items.source: Web search: DeKalb County is in Georgia

pi_items.metadata_value: DIBELS ORF (Dynamic Indicators of Basic Early Literacy Skills - Oral Reading Fluency)
pi_items.verified_value: DIBELS ORF is a real, widely-used one-minute reading fluency assessment for K-8 students developed at University of Oregon
pi_items.source: Web search: https://dibels.uoregon.edu/ confirms DIBELS is a real assessment

pi_items.verified_value: 4th grade DIBELS 8th Edition benchmark goals show beginning-of-year range 62-86 wpm (core support). 52 wpm is below beginning-of-year benchmark but realistic for a struggling reader.
pi_items.source: Web search: DIBELS 8th Edition benchmark goals show 62-86 wpm for 4th grade beginning of year

pi_items.verified_value: Woodcock-Johnson IV is a real, standardized comprehensive assessment tool for cognitive ability and achievement used in educational and clinical settings
pi_items.source: Web search: Multiple sources confirm WJ-IV is a real assessment with mean 100, SD 15

pi_items.metadata_value: Woodcock-Johnson Broad Reading Standard Score 78 (claimed 7th percentile)
pi_items.verified_value: Standard Score 78 with mean 100, SD 15 = (78-100)/15 = -1.47 SD, which corresponds to approximately the 7th percentile. This is mathematically correct.
pi_items.source: Standard normal distribution calculation: WJ-IV documentation confirms mean 100, SD 15 for standard scores

pi_items.metadata_value: Woodcock-Johnson Broad Math Standard Score 94 (claimed 34th percentile)
pi_items.verified_value: Standard Score 94 with mean 100, SD 15 = (94-100)/15 = -0.4 SD, which corresponds to approximately the 34th percentile. This is mathematically correct and falls in the 'Average' classification (90-110 range).
pi_items.source: Standard normal distribution calculation: WJ-IV documentation confirms score interpretation

pi_items.metadata_value: 8 required sections: Student Information, Present Levels (PLAAFP), Measurable Annual Goals, Special Education Services, Accommodations, Least Restrictive Environment, Assessment Participation, IEP Team Signatures
pi_items.verified_value: IDEA requires IEPs include: present levels of achievement and functional performance, measurable annual goals, special education and related services, program modifications, statement about participation with non-disabled students. Task's 8-section structure is appropriate and comprehensive.
Task requirement for document structure. 8 main sections would require at least 6-8 Heading 1 entries.
Task requirement for document structure. Subsections like individual goals would require Heading 2 styles.

pi_items.metadata_value: Minimum 3 tables required (for Annual Goals, Services List, Accommodations)
pi_items.verified_value: Task requirement. The input data includes lists of annual_goals (4 items), services (4 items), and accommodations (6 items) that are naturally suited for table format.
Task requirement for auto-generated Table of Contents
Task requirement for footer with page numbers

privileged_info_summary: The task creates a synthetic IEP (Individualized Education Program) for a fictional 4th-grade student named Mateo Reyes-Contreras using realistic assessment data. All data is synthetic, generated by the setup script from student_iep_data.json. The school district (DeKalb County School District, Georgia) is real and serves 92,000+ students. However, the school name 'Lakewood Heights Elementary School' is problematic: while it was a real school, it was actually part of Atlanta Public Schools (not DeKalb County) and was shuttered in 2004. Assessment tools referenced are authentic and widely-used: DIBELS ORF (Dynamic Indicators of Basic Early Literacy Skills - Oral Reading Fluency) and Woodcock-Johnson WJ-IV. The assessment scores are realistic and correctly interpreted—a 4th grader scoring 52 wpm on DIBELS ORF is below benchmark (62-86 wpm) but appropriate for a student with reading disabilities; the claimed WJ-IV percentiles (SS 78 = 7th percentile, SS 94 = 34th percentile) are mathematically accurate. The required IEP sections align with IDEA requirements for comprehensive IEP documentation.
